codeReview.summary: The new websocket runtime removes the old HTTP fallback, but the provider-default logic still fails the feature's explicit-override requirement. Two public override paths in `OpenAIProvider` are bypassed once shared defaults are set, so callers cannot reliably force Chat Completions or force HTTP transport.

issues.description: `resolved_api()` gives `get_use_responses_by_default()` precedence over the provider's explicit `with_api(...)` choice. After `set_use_responses_by_default(true)`, `OpenAIProvider::new().with_api(OpenAIApi::ChatCompletions)` still resolves to Responses, which breaks the feature contract that shared defaults remain overridable per provider. Upstream JS/Python providers use explicit provider options before global defaults (`reference/openai-agents-js/packages/agents-openai/src/openaiProvider.ts:107`, `reference/openai-agents-python/src/agents/models/openai_provider.py:74-88`).

`responses_transport()` cannot express an explicit HTTP override when the shared websocket default is enabled. `use_responses_websocket` is stored as a plain `bool`, and the transport check is `self.use_responses_websocket || get_use_responses_websocket_by_default() == Some(true)`, so a caller cannot opt out of websocket mode once the global default is true. This directly violates the feature requirement to honor explicit websocket transport overrides; upstream providers treat the per-provider websocket flag as tri-state/optional so `false` overrides the default (`reference/openai-agents-js/packages/agents-openai/src/openaiProvider.ts:109`, `reference/openai-agents-python/src/agents/models/openai_provider.py:83-88`).

summary: Fail: the commit makes websocket execution real, but `OpenAIProvider` still does not honor explicit per-provider API and transport overrides once shared defaults are configured.
